mechanism: Additive anticoagulant effect + GI mucosal damage
recommendation: Avoid combination unless under specialist supervision. Monitor INR closely if unavoidable. Use gastroprotection.

mechanism: NSAID inhibits platelet function + displaces warfarin from protein binding
recommendation: Avoid. Use paracetamol for pain relief in warfarin patients.

mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9 increasing warfarin levels significantly
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 30-50%. Monitor INR every 2-3 days during course.

mechanism: Risk of lactic acidosis due to acute kidney injury from contrast
recommendation: Hold metformin 48 hours before IV contrast. Restart only after renal function confirmed normal.

mechanism: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4 increasing simvastatin plasma levels
recommendation: Cap simvastatin at 20mg/day when co-prescribed with amlodipine. Consider switching to rosuvastatin.

mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition causes massive statin accumulation
recommendation: Contraindicated. Stop simvastatin during clarithromycin course. Resume after 48 hours.

mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition increases atorvastatin levels
recommendation: Temporarily stop atorvastatin during clarithromycin course.

mechanism: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate renal clearance causing toxicity
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs in patients on methotrexate. Use paracetamol. If unavoidable: short course with monitoring.

mechanism: Antacids chelate fluoroquinolones reducing absorption by 50-90%
recommendation: Separate administration by 2 hours. Give ciprofloxacin 2h before or 6h after antacid.

mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and renal digoxin clearance
recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels and ECG.

mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal lithium clearance causing toxicity
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs in patients on lithium. Use paracetamol. Monitor lithium levels if NSAID unavoidable.

mechanism: Dual RAAS blockade causes dangerous hyperkalaemia
recommendation: Avoid routine potassium supplementation in ACE inhibitor patients. Monitor electrolytes.

mechanism: Potentiates lactic acidosis risk
recommendation: Advise against heavy alcohol use. Moderate drinking (1-2 units) generally acceptable.

mechanism: Additive ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity
recommendation: Avoid combination. If unavoidable: monitor renal function and audiometry.

mechanism: Enzyme induction reduces OCP efficacy
recommendation: Counsel on contraceptive failure risk. Use alternative non-hormonal contraception or higher-dose OCP.

mechanism: Serotonin syndrome risk + SSRI inhibits tramadol activation via CYP2D6
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use opioid alternatives. If unavoidable: monitor closely for serotonin syndrome signs.

mechanism: Dual antiplatelet therapy significantly increases bleeding risk
recommendation: Only co-prescribe under cardiology guidance (post-ACS). Do not prescribe routinely in primary care.

mechanism: Rifampicin strongly induces CYP3A4 reducing amlodipine efficacy
recommendation: Expect significant BP increase. Monitor BP closely. May need dose adjustment or drug switch.

mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits warfarin metabolism via CYP1A2
recommendation: Monitor INR within 3-5 days of starting ciprofloxacin. Anticipate INR rise of 30-50%.

mechanism: Antacids reduce phenytoin absorption
recommendation: Separate phenytoin and antacid administration by 2-3 hours.

mechanism: Trimethoprim inhibits renal creatinine secretion falsely elevating creatinine
recommendation: Monitor renal function when prescribing trimethoprim to metformin patients.

mechanism: Combined negative chronotropic and inotropic effect: severe bradycardia and heart block
recommendation: Contraindicated in combination. Never co-prescribe without specialist supervision.

mechanism: Dual RAAS: severe hyperkalaemia risk
recommendation: Avoid in primary care without specialist input and close electrolyte monitoring.

mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9 increasing sulphonylurea levels: hypoglycaemia risk
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely. Consider temporary dose reduction of glibenclamide.

mechanism: Profound hypotension — potentially fatal
recommendation: Absolute contraindication. Sildenafil must not be taken within 24 hours of nitrates.

mechanism: Additive CNS and respiratory depression: risk of fatal respiratory arrest
recommendation: Avoid combination whenever possible. If required: use lowest effective doses. Patient must be counselled.

mechanism: Fluconazole strongly inhibits CYP2C9 causing marked INR elevation
recommendation: Monitor INR within 2-3 days. Expect 50-100% INR rise. Reduce warfarin dose proactively.

mechanism: Calcium chelation reduces tetracycline absorption by 50-80%
recommendation: Take tetracycline 1 hour before or 2 hours after dairy products.

mechanism: Serotonin impairs platelet aggregation + NSAID GI mucosal damage: GI bleed risk
recommendation: Avoid combination if possible. Add PPI gastroprotection if combination unavoidable.

mechanism: Both are folate antagonists: combined bone marrow suppression and mucositis
recommendation: Contraindicated. Use alternative antibiotics in patients on methotrexate.
